overall_summary: This batch of articles covers sports news and a climate-related lawsuit. The sports stories include a record-breaking NBA performance, a preview of the Women's Asian Cup final, and a report about an Iranian footballer's expulsion from the national team. The non-sports article details a lawsuit by Pakistani farmers against German companies over the 2022 floods.

articles.title: Luka Doncic scores 60 points as Lakers rally to defeat Heat in NBA | Basketball News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Luka Doncic scored 60 points, setting a record for the most points ever scored against the Miami Heat, to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 134-126 road victory. The Lakers, who won their eighth straight game, also got a triple-double from LeBron James. The Heat, missing two key injured players, led early but were outscored in the second half.

articles.title: Why Pakistani farmers are suing two German companies for deadly 2022 floods | Climate Crisis | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Thirty-nine Pakistani farmers from Sindh province are suing two German companies, RWE and Heidelberg Materials, alleging their greenhouse gas emissions contributed to the catastrophic 2022 floods. The floods, described as a climate-induced disaster, killed over 1,700 people and caused an estimated $40bn in damages. The farmers' case relates to the companies being among major industrial producers of historical carbon emissions.

articles.title: Japan vs Australia: Women’s Asian Cup final – team news, start and lineups | AFC Asian Cup News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Hosts Australia will face Japan in the AFC Women's Asian Cup final at Stadium Australia in Sydney. Australia, seeking their first major trophy and redemption after a 2023 World Cup semifinal loss at the same venue, has lost both previous Asian Cup finals to Japan. Japan, the highest-ranked team, has been dominant in the tournament with a perfect record and clean sheets leading to the final.

articles.title: Iran footballer Azmoun kicked off national team for disloyalty, say reports | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Reports from Iranian media state that striker Sardar Azmoun has been expelled from the national team for a perceived act of disloyalty. The reports link the expulsion to an Instagram post showing Azmoun with Dubai's ruler, which drew criticism from state TV pundits. His absence would weaken Iran's squad ahead of the upcoming FIFA World Cup, though the national football federation has not officially commented.

overall_summary: The articles cover a range of international news from the last six hours, including military incidents in the US-Iran conflict, domestic US legal and political developments, FIFA disciplinary and financial news, ongoing violence in Gaza, a sports controversy in Africa, a cartel raid in Mexico, and a US commemorative coin approval.

articles.title: US F-35 aircraft makes emergency landing after a combat mission over Iran | US-Israel war on Iran News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A US F-35 fighter jet made an emergency landing at a Middle Eastern airbase after a combat mission over Iran, landing safely with the pilot in stable condition. US Central Command confirmed the incident is under investigation. CNN reported, citing anonymous sources, that the aircraft was likely hit by Iran, and Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed it targeted a US aircraft, though the US has not confirmed the cause.

articles.title: US states file lawsuit challenging Trump’s revocation of climate finding | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: A coalition of 23 US states, led by California and New York, has filed a legal petition challenging the Trump administration's revocation of the 'endangerment finding,' a key scientific determination that climate change threatens human health and the environment. The lawsuit, filed in the US Court of Appeals, also challenges the repeal of tailpipe emissions standards for certain vehicle model years. The states argue the revocation unlawfully removes foundational protections for addressing climate change.

articles.title: FIFA fines Israel FA citing racism and ‘multiple’ discrimination breaches | Football News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA has fined the Israeli Football Association (IFA) 150,000 Swiss francs for multiple breaches of its anti-discrimination obligations, citing a failure to take meaningful action against Beitar Jerusalem for persistent racist behavior. The disciplinary committee highlighted 'deficient and substantively inadequate' sanctions against the club. In a separate investigation, FIFA announced no action would be taken against the IFA over allegations concerning Israeli clubs based in the occupied West Bank.

articles.title: FIFA details Infantino’s $6m deal with 33% Club World Cup bonus increase | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: FIFA President Gianni Infantino received a 33% increase in his annual bonus last year, part of a $6 million pay package, as detailed in FIFA's latest accounts. His bonus rose to 2.2 million Swiss francs, linked to the first monthlong men's Club World Cup. FIFA also set a budget target of $14 billion in revenue for the 2027-30 cycle, which includes future World Cups and development allocations.

articles.title: Israel continues Gaza attacks amid regional war, kills several Palestinians | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes in Gaza City killed at least three Palestinians and wounded others, according to doctors at al-Shifa Hospital. The attacks occurred as the Rafah border crossing with Egypt reopened for limited medical evacuations after a 19-day closure. The Palestinian Red Crescent Society stated only eight wounded Palestinians and 17 family members would be allowed into Egypt for treatment.

articles.title: Senegal and Morocco tied by religion and trade but divided by AFCON fallout | Africa Cup of Nations News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) overturned Senegal's Africa Cup of Nations victory and awarded the title to Morocco, a decision met with disbelief in Senegal. Senegal's government says it will pursue legal avenues and called for an investigation into suspected corruption. The move strains the close ties between the two nations, which are built on shared religion, trade, and cultural exchanges.

articles.title: Mexican military says 11 killed in raid targeting Sinaloa cartel leader | Crime News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: Mexican authorities say 11 people were killed during a raid in Culiacan that resulted in the capture of Omar Oswaldo Torres, leader of the Los Mayos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel. The Mexican Navy stated its personnel were attacked and returned fire, killing the assailants, and seized high-powered weapons. The raid is part of broader regional efforts against cartels, though experts warn such 'decapitation strategies' can increase long-term violence.

articles.title: US arts commission approves gold coin stamped with Donald Trump’s face | Donald Trump News | Al Jazeera
articles.summary: The US Commission of Fine Arts, composed of Trump appointees, unanimously approved plans for a commemorative gold coin featuring a portrait of Donald Trump. The coin may sidestep federal law prohibiting living presidents on currency, as it is commemorative and not for circulation. The Trump administration has also advanced plans to put the president's face on a $1 coin, which critics denounce as unlawful and inappropriate for a democracy.
